symptoms.symptom_name: Continuous focal (right-sided) clonic twitching for ~3 hours
symptoms.character: rhythmic focal motor twitching of the right upper limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A focal motor seizure continuing beyond 5 minutes without recovery IS status epilepticus by definition.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regurgitation of all enteral feeds with a wet, gurgling cough
symptoms.character: wet gurgling cough, bringing up all feed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regurgitation with a wet cough in a dysphagic tube-fed patient is a direct aspiration signature.
A new depressed conscious state suggests ongoing (possibly non-convulsive) seizure activity and/or hypoxia.
Hypoxia from aspiration also depresses conscious state.

loinc_ref.display: Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hypoxia — clinician-measured, sealed; the AI infers respiratory compromise from the carer's report of fast, noisy, dusky breathing, not a measured number.

symptoms.symptom_name: Decreased air entry at the right lung base with transmitted upper-airway rhonchi

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Basal reduced air entry localises aspiration; auscultation is a clinician-only finding, sealed and impossible in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fast, noisy breathing with dusky colour (carer-observed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachypnoea and dusky colour are carer-observable proxies for hypoxia and respiratory distress.
Loss of airway protection with pooled secretions raises aspiration risk; partly video-observable, partly examination.
Systemic stress/early inflammatory response; clinician-measured vitals, sealed.
Dysphagia is the standing risk factor that makes aspiration the expected complication of a feeding event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known chronic epilepsy on regular anticonvulsants (baseline)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-existing epilepsy is the substrate for a breakthrough prolonged seizure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of high fever and nuchal rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No high fever or neck stiffness makes primary CNS infection less likely, but it cannot be fully excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bedside blood glucose (to exclude hypoglycaemia as a seizure trigger)

investigation_details.expected_result: Unknown — must be checked in any prolonged seizure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A treatable hypoglycaemic seizure trigger could be missed; the AI cannot obtain this remotely, which is itself a reason to escalate.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypoglycaemia or metabolic derangement as a seizure trigger
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bedside glucose is mandatory in any prolonged seizure and is a clinician/hospital measurement here.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A prolonged unremitting focal seizure with depressed consciousness in a patient with known epilepsy defines a neurological emergency.
Regurgitation-associated wet cough, pooled secretions, basal signs and hypoxia in a dysphagic patient define an airway/respiratory emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer for airway assessment, oxygen and suction
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The airway and seizure emergencies require hospital-level intervention now; this supersedes any home investigation.
Mandatory in any prolonged seizure to exclude a treatable hypoglycaemic trigger — to be done by paramedics/hospital.
Confirms aspiration and its extent — hospital investigation, not available in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home coaching to continue enteral feeding
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Feeds must be stopped, not continued; ongoing feeding worsens aspiration.
